meta.title: Bohai Rim
meta.subtitle: Industry-cluster overview
meta.tagline: China's northern workshop rings a gulf, not a river — heavy industry, brand champions and a hidden belt of one-county-one-product towns. This is the cluster map around the Bohai Sea.
meta.methodology: Place coordinates are from Google Places, converted from China's GCJ-02 datum to WGS-84 to sit correctly on the OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap. Each pin marks a place's dominant manufacturing or trade specialty drawn from current sourcing guides; clusters are indicative, not exclusive, and shift over time. Scope is the broad Bohai Economic Rim (Beijing, Tianjin, Hebei, Shandong, Liaoning); Jing-Jin-Ji is its core.

clusters.note: China's tech and R&D core — software, AI and chip design; the old electronics malls gave way to startups.
Beijing Economic-Technological Development Area — chips (SMIC), displays (BOE), robotics and EVs.
Petrochemicals, Airbus A320 final assembly, autos and offshore equipment; the great northern port.
China's bicycle and e-bike capital; also autos and parts.
China's steel capital — more steel than almost any country; also rail stock, ceramics and robots.
Great Wall Motors' home and 'China Electric Valley' — solar (Yingli), wind and power equipment.
China's luggage and bag capital — a vast production base and wholesale market.
The wire-mesh capital — 6,700+ factories and over half of China's wire-mesh exports.
One of China's largest leather and fur garment bases and markets.
China's towel capital — towels, bath textiles and bedding.
China's cashmere capital — the world's largest cashmere processing and trading hub.
Flat-glass and glassware capital.
Provincial capital — pharmaceuticals (CSPC) and textiles.
Pipe fittings, casting and auto dies and moulds.
Home-appliance capital — Haier, Hisense; plus tires, trains (CRRC Sifang), beer and a top-10 port.
Diesel engines (Weichai), agricultural machinery and acoustics/MEMS (Goertek).
Heavy trucks (Sinotruk), heavy industry and software.
Ceramics, petrochemicals and new materials.
Autos and parts, wine and electronics; gold processing at nearby Zhaoyuan.
Fishing-tackle and outdoor-gear capital; swimwear, tires and marine industries.
Northern China's wholesale-commodity capital — the 'Yiwu of the north'.
Shengli oilfield and the Yellow River Delta — petrochemicals, rubber and oil equipment.
China's 'Solar Valley' — solar water heaters and renewable-energy equipment (Himin).
Heavy machinery, machine tools, aerospace and autos (BMW Brilliance).
Shipbuilding, the great northeastern port, petrochemicals and IT/software outsourcing.
Steel city — Ansteel, one of China's oldest iron-and-steel bases.
China's bearing capital.

orientation.v: R&D, chips and services in Beijing; petrochem, aerospace, autos and the port in Tianjin — the Jing-Jin-Ji heart.
Tangshan steel plus a belt of one-county-one-product towns: wire mesh (Anping), bags (Baigou), leather/fur (Xinji), towels (Gaoyang), cashmere (Qinghe).
Qingdao appliances, Weifang engines, Jinan trucks, Zibo chemicals, Linyi wholesale — heavy and brand-led.
Shenyang machinery, Dalian shipbuilding, Anshan steel, Wafangdian bearings — China's first heavy-industry heartland.
More state-owned and heavy than the southern deltas, but Hebei's county clusters rival anything in Zhejiang.

notes: It is a rim, not a delta: industry rings the Bohai gulf — Liaoning to the north, Hebei and Tianjin to the west, Shandong to the south.

The north skews heavier and more state-owned than the deltas — steel (Tangshan, Anshan), engines (Weifang), trucks (Jinan), ships (Dalian), chemicals — alongside brand champions like Haier and Great Wall.
Yet Hebei hides true single-product clusters every bit as specialised as Zhejiang's: Anping makes the world's wire mesh, Qinghe its cashmere, Baigou its bags, Gaoyang its towels.
Jing-Jin-Ji (Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei) is the official megaregion at the core; Shandong and Liaoning complete the wider Bohai Economic Rim.
